zh.Hailes, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾海利斯
zh.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlan於大約1260年出生在英國賀福爾維格莫。她於大約1284年與Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock在英國賀福爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1316年10月6日之後在英國奧可福爾康薇爾，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾海利斯。

zh-hans.Hailes, Gloucestershire, England: 英国格盧斯特斯爾海利斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlan于大约1260年出生在英国賀福爾維格莫。她于大约1284年与Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock在英国賀福爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1316年10月6日之后在英国奧可福爾康薇爾，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾海利斯。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlan was born about 1260, in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England. She married Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock about 1284, in Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died after 6 October 1316, in Cornwell, Oxfordshire, England, and was buried in Hailes, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlan est née environ 1260, à Wigmore, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock environ 1284, à Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 6 octobre 1316, à Cornwell, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Hailes, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlan wurde um 1260 in Wigmore, Herefordshire, England geboren. Sie hat um 1284 in Herefordshire, England Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 6. Oktober 1316 in Cornwell, Oxfordshire, England und wurde in Hailes, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlan è nata il circa 1260 nel Wigmore, Herefordshire, England. Lei sposato Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock circa 1284, nel Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 6 ottobre 1316, nel Cornwell, Oxfordshire, England, e fu sepolta a Hailes, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlanは約1260年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアウィグモーで生まれた。約1284年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアでSir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnockと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1316年10月6日以後にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアコルンウエルで死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアヘイレスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlan(은)는 대략 1260년 영국 허퍼드실르 위그모어에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1284년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1316년 10월 6일 이후 영국 스퍼드실르 코른웰에서 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 하일레스에 묻혔다.

mn.about 1260–after 6 October 1316: ойролцоогоор 1260 он–1316 оны Аравдугаар сарын 6–наас хойш

pt.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlan nasceu aproximadamente 1260, em Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock aproximadamente 1284, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 6 de outubro de 1316, em Cornwell, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Hailes, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlan родилась около 1260 г. в местности вигмор, хирефордшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock около 1284 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 6 октябрь 1316 г. в местности корнвелл, оксфордшир, Англия и была похоронена в местности хеилесь, глоусестиршир, Англия.

sm.about 1260–after 6 October 1316: e tusa o le 1260–ina ua ma'ea le 6 Oketopa 1316

es.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlan nació aproximadamente 1260, en Wigmore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Sir Richard de Cornwall , Lord of Thunnock aproximadamente 1284, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió después de 6 de octubre de 1316, en Cornwell, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Hailes, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
